**3. Tables:**

Table: (Based on text)

Headers:
*   Fuel thermal input
*   Furnace temperature
*   Fuel feeding rate
*   Elec. Heating energy

Data:
*   max. 50 kW
*   max. 1600 °C
*   1 - 7 kg/h
*   38 kW

**4. Diagrams:**

Components and labels (as seen in Fig. 1):

*   **Small Scale Vertical Combustion Chamber**
    *   Fuel Feeder (1.)
    *   Radiative section (electrically heated)
    *   Burner (2.)
        *   Primary air + Pulverised fuel
        *   Secondary air
        *   Port 1, Residence time: 0.3 s
        *   Port 2, Residence time: 0.6 s
        *   Port 3, Residence time: 1.8 s
    *   Furnace (3.)
        *   Convective section (water cooled)
    *   Quench system (4.)
    *   Cyclone (5.)
        *   Ash
    *   Filter (6.)
    *   Vacuum unit / Blower (7.)
    *   Atmosphere
    *   Pressurized Air
*   **Burner design**
    *   Natural gas
    *   Pulverised fuel + Primary air
    *   Secondary air
    *   Movable block swirl generator
    * Burner quarl

**5. Key numbers, constants, parameters:**

*   Radiative section length: 2.2 m
*   Radiative section diameter: 0.3 m
*   Convective section length: 1.8 m
*   Convective section diameter: 0.25 m
*   Heating element thermal input: 8.8 kW each
*   Heating element temperature withstand: up to 1600 °C
*   Offgas target temperature: Below 1000°C
*   Quench system cools offgas to: about 180 °C
*   RDF fuel-nitrogen content: 0.76%
*   RDF sulfur content: 0.04%
*   Middleburg coal particle size distribution: 75% < 75 µm
*   Hambach coal particle size distribution: 75% < 150 µm
*   RDF particle sizes used: Smaller than 0.5 mm and not larger than 1 mm
*   Burner fuel input: 15 kW

**Units:**

*   m
*   °C
*   kg/h
*   kW
*   µm
*   s

*   Injection velocity of RDF: 35 m/

title: refuse_derived_fuel
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 4]
Here is the breakdown of technical content found in the provided image:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   b  =  (1 - ash0/ash1) / (1- ash0)

**2. Graphs:**

*   **Figure 4: Temperature vs Distance**
    *   Axes:
        *   Y-axis: Temperature, °C
        *   X-axis: Distance from the burner, m
    *   Curves:
        *   RDF < 1mm: B-1
        *   RDF < 0.5mm: B-1
        *   Middelburg: B-1
        *   Middelburg: B-2
        *   Hambach: B-2

*   **Figure 5: O2 Concentration vs Distance**
    *   Axes:
        *   Y-axis: O2 concentration, Vol.-%
        *   X-axis: Distance from the burner, m
    *   Curves:
        *   RDF < 1mm: B-1
        *   RDF < 0.5mm: B-1
        *   Middelburg: B-1
        *   Middelburg: B-2
        *   Hambach: B-2

*   **Figure 6: Burnout vs Distance**
    *   Axes:
        *   Y-axis: Burnout (%)
        *   X-axis: Distance from the burner, m
    *   Curves:
        *   RDF < 1mm: B-1
        *   RDF < 0.5mm: B-1
        *   Middelburg: B-1
        *   Middelburg: B-2
        *   Hambach: B-2

*   **Figure 7: Carbon in Ash vs Fuel Type**
    *   Axes:
        *   Y-axis: Carbon in ash (%)
        *   X-axis: RDF<1mm B-1, RDF<0.5mm B-1, Middelburg B-1, Middelburg B-2, Hambach B-2
    *   Curves:
        *   Bar Chart

**3. Tables:**

NO_TABLE_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_DIAGRAM_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Excess air ratio: 1.2
*   Oxygen mole fraction in combustion products: 4% (dry)
*   Coal flame temperature range: 1300-1330 °C
*   RDF flame temperature range: 200-300 °C lower than coal flames
*   RDF Flame temperatures increase to 1050 - 1100°C
*   Coal flame decrease from 1350 - 1200 °C.
*   Fine RDF maximum flame temperature occurs 1.1m downstream
*   Coal flame O2 concentration: 4-6%
*   RDF flame O2 concentration: 12-16%
*   Residence time at 4m downstream: 1.8 s
*   O2 at 4m downstream: 6%
*   RDF burnout at furnace end: 93% (coarse), 97% (fine)
*   Coal flame burnout: 99.5%
*   RDF Volatile Matter (ASTM): 76.59%
*   Particle Size: 500-1000 µm
*   Heat flux: 200 kW/m2
*   Particle Heating rate: 10-20 °C/

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Coke Yield: 15-20%
*   RDF Volatiles Calorific Value: 16-18 MJ/kg
*   LCV of Raw RDF: 24 MJ/kg
*   RDF Chemical Enthalpy Released with Volatiles: 70-80%
*   RDF tars destroyed in 1200-1400°C flame temp
*   High Temp Volatile Matter Yield (Sub-bituminous Coal): 60%
*   Calorific Value (Sub-bituminous Coal): 18 MJ/kg
*   High Temp Volatile Matter Yield (Bituminous Coal): 43%
*   LCV of Volatiles: 40 MJ/kg
*   Volatiles Generation Rate: 4-6 K per minute
*   Middelburg Coal (daf), gaseous mass: 16.7%
    *   5.1% CO, 4.2% CH4, 3.8% CO2, 1.3% N2, 1.3% H2, 0.1% C2H4, 0.6% C2H6, 0.2% C2H8, 0.12% O2
*   Middelburg Coal,condensable mass: 15%
    *   7.2% pyrolysis water, 7.8% tars
*   RDF, (daf), gaseous mass: 21.4%
    *   4.15% CO, 3.77% CH4 , 8.54% CO2 , 1.08% N2, 0.76% H2 , 1.05% C2H4 , 1.29% C2H6 , 0.5% C2H8, 0.24% O2
*   RDF, condensable mass: 46.4%
    *   13.6% water, 32.8% tars
*   Flame NO emission (Coal Flames): 600-700 ppm at Port 1
*   Flame NO emission (RDF Flames): 200 to 500-600 ppm
*   CO concentration (Coal Flames): 2000 ppm (vol., dry) at Port 1 to few ppm at Port 4
*   CO concentration (RDF Flames): >2000 ppm
*   Ash Deposit Thickness: 15 mm
*   Ash Deposit Carbon Content
